questions.text: I hold the view that feeling things intensely is simply how I'm wired.
I hold the view that putting others first is simply the right way to live.
I find that other people's gratitude means more to me than any personal reward.
I find that the moral demand of suffering grows stronger, not weaker, the more carefully I consider it.
I prefer to formulate my response to a difficult human situation before engaging with its emotional content.
I find that I am surprised when people read a lot into minor changes in my behavior.
I experience nostalgia as a pleasant memory rather than an aching longing.
I feel responsible for keeping a group on track toward its goals.
I find that self-directed effort is the most energizing kind of work.
I feel that waiting for full consensus wastes valuable momentum.
I prefer flexible timelines that the group agrees upon together.
I can detect insincerity in someone's tone of voice even over the phone.
I hold the view that the moral weight of suffering should not be adjusted downward because of how common or expected the suffering is.
I find that being thorough the first time saves me from having to redo work later.
I feel more in control when I'm free to react to what's actually happening rather than following a script.
I feel that the future is something you design through deliberate action in the present.
I tend to go all-in emotionally, whether the feeling is positive or negative.
I often notice when someone's enthusiasm is performed rather than felt.
